title: bossiaea aquifolium subsp. laidlawiana
intro: Bossiaea aquifolium subsp. laidlawiana, also known as Laidlaw's Bossiaea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it has been used to treat a range of health issues. The leaves and stems of Laidlaw's Bossiaea have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, and aid in the healing of wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tea or tincture, and is believed to provide health benefits such as reduced fever, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
